title: Interpret VISU_MDS Machine Data IDs and Identify Which Values Are Expected to Be Unique
summary: Use the VISU_MDS Machine Data screen and Table 4-10 to identify a machine data field by ID and determine whether the source treats that value as one that should match from machine to machine or as one of the documented unique exceptions.
when_to_use: Use when reviewing a value on the VISU_MDS Machine Data screen and you need to identify the field by ID and interpret whether the value is generally expected to match across machines or is documented as unique for each machine and axis.
not_for: Do not use for IDs that are not clearly listed in the provided source material.
Do not assign meaning to IDs marked N/A in Table 4-10.
Do not use this runbook as the operator station alignment procedure; the source only states that MD5 and MD21 are measured during alignment procedures and does not provide those procedures here.
Do not use this runbook as authorization to change Machine Data values; the related source context states these parameters generally do not require changing unless a motor is replaced.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Review the VISU_MDS Machine Data screen, identify the field by ID using Table 4-10, and classify the value as generally matching across machines or unique when the source explicitly says so.

safety_notes: This is a reference procedure for interpretation only.
VISU_MDS Machine Data parameters generally should not be changed unless a motor is replaced.
MD5 Tote Pickup Position and MD21 Home Offset are stated to be measured during operator station alignment procedures; this runbook does not include those alignment steps.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the operator station HMI

access_required: Access to the operator station HMI
system_preconditions: VISU_MDS Machine Data screen is available for review.

visual_references.description: Operator Station HMI Machine Data screen showing axis parameter settings and placement of machine data entries.
Operator station HMI reference showing navigation to the Visu_MDs screen using F2.
Reference artifact showing Home Offset use on the Visu_MDs screen during commissioning context.
Reference artifact related to Tote Pickup Position in commissioning context.
Reference artifact related to Tote Dump Position on the Visu_MDs screen.
Additional AGV/tipper HMI reference related to Tote Dump Position on the Visu_MDs screen.

steps.title: Open the VISU_MDS Machine Data screen and locate the reviewed ID
steps.purpose: Find the machine data entry being reviewed on the correct HMI screen.
steps.instruction: Open the VISU_MDS "Machine Data" screen and locate the machine data entry being reviewed by its ID number.
steps.expected_result: The reviewed machine data entry is visible on the VISU_MDS Machine Data screen and its ID can be read.
steps.healthy_condition: The correct Machine Data screen is open and the target ID is identifiable on the displayed list of axis parameters.
steps.failure_condition: The Machine Data screen cannot be accessed, or the reviewed entry cannot be located by ID.

actions.label: Locate reviewed ID
actions.exact_action: Locate the machine data entry being reviewed by its ID number.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Machine Data screen layout and machine data entries for axes such as Z1, A1, Z2, and A2.
Operator station HMI context showing navigation to the Visu_MDs screen using F2.

stop_or_escalate_if: The reviewed ID is not clearly listed in the provided source material.
The VISU_MDS Machine Data screen cannot be accessed or the entry cannot be confidently identified.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 4-26, titled "Operator Station HMI Machine Data Screen," appears on page 55 and shows the Machine Data view containing parameters for each axis: Z1, A1, Z2, and A2.
The user is instructed to navigate on the operator station HMI to the "Visu_MDs" screen using F2.

steps.title: Identify the field meaning from Table 4-10
steps.purpose: Map the displayed ID number to the documented machine data field name.
steps.instruction: Use Table 4-10 to identify the meaning of the displayed ID. The source lists fields including ID 0 Axis Name, 1 OT Minus, 5 Tote Pickup Position, 6 Plus Travel Opposite Axis Position, 7 Neg Travel Opposite Axis Position, 8 Tote Dump Position, 9 Soft Travel Lim Pos, 10 OT Pos, 11 Base Speed for AutoCycle, 12 Base Acc/Dec for AutoCycle, 13 Jog Speed, 14 Jog Acceleration, 16 Tote Prox Simulation, 20 Home Position, 21 Home Offset, 22 Homing Speed, 23 Homing CAM Speed, and 24 Homing Acceleration.
steps.expected_result: The reviewed ID is matched to a documented field name from Table 4-10.
steps.healthy_condition: The ID corresponds to a field explicitly listed in the source table.
steps.failure_condition: The ID is not listed, or the table marks the entry N/A so the field meaning cannot be supported from this source.

actions.label: Cross-reference ID to table
actions.exact_action: Use Table 4-10 to identify the meaning of the displayed ID.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Machine Data entry being reviewed so its ID can be cross-referenced to Table 4-10.

stop_or_escalate_if: The reviewed ID is not clearly listed in the provided source material.
The table marks the reviewed ID as N/A.
The field meaning cannot be supported directly from Table 4-10.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: From machine to machine, the following data values should match: ID 0 Axis Name, 1 OT Minus, 5 Tote Pickup Position, 6 Plus Travel Opposite Axis Position, 7 Neg Travel Opposite Axis Position, 8 Tote Dump Position, 9 Soft Travel Lim Pos, 10 OT Pos, 11 Base Speed for AutoCycle, 12 Base Acc/Dec for AutoCycle, 13 Jog Speed, 14 Jog Acceleration.
Table 4-10 continues with ID 16 Tote Prox Simulation, 20 Home Position, 21 Home Offset, 22 Homing Speed, 23 Homing CAM Speed, and 24 Homing Acceleration; several other IDs are marked N/A.

steps.title: Classify MD5 and MD21 as unique exceptions
steps.purpose: Apply the source-documented exception rule for the two machine data values that are not expected to match across machines.
steps.instruction: If the reviewed field is MD5 Tote Pickup Position or MD21 Home Offset, interpret it as unique for every machine and every axis.
steps.expected_result: MD5 and MD21 are recognized as unique values rather than match-across-machine values.
steps.healthy_condition: The reviewed field is correctly identified as one of the two documented exceptions and is treated as unique per machine and per axis.
steps.failure_condition: MD5 or MD21 is incorrectly treated as a value that should match from machine to machine.

actions.label: Apply uniqueness exception
actions.exact_action: If the reviewed field is MD5 Tote Pickup Position or MD21 Home Offset, interpret it as unique for every machine and every axis.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The reviewed Machine Data entry after identifying whether it is MD5 Tote Pickup Position or MD21 Home Offset.
Home Offset reference on the Visu_MDs screen in commissioning context.
Tote Pickup Position reference in commissioning context.

stop_or_escalate_if: The reviewed field appears to be MD5 or MD21 but cannot be confirmed from the source table.
Additional alignment procedure detail is needed; this section does not provide the operator station alignment procedure.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: There are two exceptions for matching values: MD5 Tote Pickup Position will be unique for every machine and every axis. MD21 Home Offset will be unique for every machine and every axis.
These values are both measured during the operator station alignment procedures.

steps.title: Classify other documented listed values as match-across-machine values
steps.purpose: Apply the source rule for the documented machine data values that should match from machine to machine, except for the stated exceptions.
steps.instruction: If the reviewed field is one of the other documented machine data values in this section, interpret it as a value that should match from machine to machine. Do not override the explicit exceptions for MD5 Tote Pickup Position and MD21 Home Offset.
steps.expected_result: The reviewed field is classified as a generally matching value when it is one of the documented listed values other than the two exceptions.
steps.healthy_condition: A documented listed value other than MD5 or MD21 is treated as a match-across-machine value.
steps.failure_condition: A listed non-exception value is treated as unique without source support, or an N/A entry is assigned a meaning.

actions.label: Apply matching-values rule
actions.exact_action: If the reviewed field is one of the other documented machine data values in the section, interpret it as a value that should match from machine to machine.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The reviewed Machine Data entry after identifying its field name from the listed values.
Tote Dump Position reference on the Visu_MDs screen in commissioning context.
Additional Tote Dump Position HMI context tied to Visu_MDs.

stop_or_escalate_if: The reviewed ID is not one of the documented listed values.
The reviewed entry is marked N/A in Table 4-10.
The source does not clearly support whether the value should match or be unique.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: From machine to machine, the following data values should match: ID 0 Axis Name, 1 OT Minus, 5 Tote Pickup Position, 6 Plus Travel Opposite Axis Position, 7 Neg Travel Opposite Axis Position, 8 Tote Dump Position, 9 Soft Travel Lim Pos, 10 OT Pos, 11 Base Speed for AutoCycle, 12 Base Acc/Dec for AutoCycle, 13 Jog Speed, 14 Jog Acceleration.
Table 4-10 continues with ID 16 Tote Prox Simulation, 20 Home Position, 21 Home Offset, 22 Homing Speed, 23 Homing CAM Speed, and 24 Homing Acceleration; several other IDs are marked N/A.
There are two exceptions for matching values: MD5 Tote Pickup Position will be unique for every machine and every axis. MD21 Home Offset will be unique for every machine and every axis.

steps.title: Record the field name and interpretation
steps.purpose: Capture the reviewed field identity and the source-supported interpretation for later review or comparison.
steps.instruction: Record the field name and whether the source treats it as a matching value or a unique value.
steps.expected_result: A documented record exists showing the reviewed field name and its interpretation category.
steps.healthy_condition: The record clearly states the ID, field name, and whether it is treated as matching across machines or unique per machine and axis.
steps.failure_condition: The interpretation is not recorded, or the record includes unsupported meaning for an unlisted or N/A ID.

actions.label: Document interpretation
actions.exact_action: Record the field name and whether the source treats it as a matching value or a unique value.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The reviewed Machine Data entry while documenting the ID and field name.

stop_or_escalate_if: The field name cannot be supported from the source.
The value classification cannot be supported from the source.
The reviewed ID is unlisted or marked N/A.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: From machine to machine, the following data values should match: ID 0 Axis Name, 1 OT Minus, 5 Tote Pickup Position, 6 Plus Travel Opposite Axis Position, 7 Neg Travel Opposite Axis Position, 8 Tote Dump Position, 9 Soft Travel Lim Pos, 10 OT Pos, 11 Base Speed for AutoCycle, 12 Base Acc/Dec for AutoCycle, 13 Jog Speed, 14 Jog Acceleration.
Table 4-10 continues with ID 16 Tote Prox Simulation, 20 Home Position, 21 Home Offset, 22 Homing Speed, 23 Homing CAM Speed, and 24 Homing Acceleration; several other IDs are marked N/A.
There are two exceptions for matching values: MD5 Tote Pickup Position will be unique for every machine and every axis. MD21 Home Offset will be unique for every machine and every axis.

success_criteria: The reviewed VISU_MDS Machine Data entry is identified by ID using the source-supported table.
The field name is mapped to a documented Table 4-10 entry.
The value is correctly interpreted as either generally matching across machines or as one of the two documented unique exceptions.
The interpretation is recorded without assigning unsupported meaning to N/A or unlisted IDs.
healthy_conditions: The VISU_MDS Machine Data screen is available and the reviewed ID is visible.
The reviewed ID maps directly to a documented field in Table 4-10.
MD5 Tote Pickup Position and MD21 Home Offset are treated as unique for every machine and every axis.
Other documented listed values are treated as values that should match from machine to machine.
failure_conditions: The reviewed ID is not clearly listed in the provided source material.
The reviewed entry is marked N/A in Table 4-10.
A user assigns meaning or classification not supported by the source.
A user attempts to derive operator station alignment steps from this section even though the procedure is not provided here.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the reviewed ID is not clearly listed in the provided source material.
Escalate if the reviewed entry is marked N/A and additional interpretation is requested.

Escalate if alignment-procedure detail is needed for MD5 Tote Pickup Position or MD21 Home Offset, because this section only states those values are measured during operator station alignment procedures.
Escalate if a parameter change is being considered; related source context states these values generally do not require changing unless a motor is replaced.

Table 4-10 continues with ID 16 Tote Prox Simulation, 20 Home Position, 21 Home Offset, 22 Homing Speed, 23 Homing CAM Speed, and 24 Homing Acceleration; several other IDs are marked N/A.
